hero.question: Will Ubisoft be acquired before 2027?
hero.prob_take: At 33%, the market is pricing in a roughly 1-in-3 shot that Tencent or a mega-acquirer actually closes a deal by 2027, down hard from last week, which means either recent noise spooked retail or smart money just closed out longs ahead of earnings. Ubisoft's stock is toxic, the French government makes any deal radioactive, and we're still 2+ years out with zero serious bidder in the room, this feels fairly valued or maybe even generous to the YES side.

daily_take.headline: Ubisoft Acquisition Odds Crater 10 Points. Smart Money Knows Why.
daily_take.deck: Ubisoft acquisition odds fell from 43% to 33% in 24 hours on $565K volume, a sharp reversal that signals new information hitting the market. The drop tracks a cooling acquisition narrative after months of restructuring noise. This is likely overcorrection. French regulatory friction and Vivendi's creeping stake create real acquisition pressure, but the market just priced in permanence. Contrarian play: 33% still undervalues a 2-3 year forced auction scenario under EU pressure.

sidebar.headline: Steyer momentum is noise. California's 2026 governor race just got real coverage and 13.4% still assumes mainstream viability he doesn't have.

sidebar.headline: SpaceX IPO odds jumped on Elon's latest restructuring talk, but $3K volume means this price is fiction. Illiquid markets reward patient capital.
